questions.question: What is matter?
questions.questionThai: สสารคืออะไร?
options: Anything that takes up space and has mass
questions.explanation: Matter is anything that takes up space and has mass, including solids, liquids, and gases.

questions.question: What are the three main states of matter?
questions.questionThai: สสารสามสถานะหลักคืออะไร?
questions.explanation: The three main states of matter are solid, liquid, and gas.

questions.question: Which state of matter has a definite shape and definite volume?
questions.questionThai: สสารสถานะใดมีรูปร่างและปริมาตรที่แน่นอน?
questions.explanation: Solids have both a definite shape and a definite volume because their particles are tightly packed.

questions.question: What is an example of a liquid?
questions.questionThai: ตัวอย่างของของเหลวคืออะไร?
questions.explanation: Water is a liquid. Ice is solid water, and steam is water as a gas.

questions.question: What happens when ice melts?
questions.questionThai: เกิดอะไรขึ้นเมื่อน้ำแข็งละลาย?
questions.explanation: When ice melts, it changes from a solid to a liquid (water).

questions.question: What causes matter to change states?
questions.questionThai: อะไรทำให้สสารเปลี่ยนสถานะ?
options: Moving it to a new place
questions.explanation: Adding or removing heat (energy) causes matter to change states.

questions.question: What is freezing?
questions.questionThai: การแข็งตัวคืออะไร?
options: When a gas becomes a liquid
When a liquid becomes a solid
When a solid becomes a gas
When a liquid becomes a gas
questions.explanation: Freezing is when a liquid changes into a solid by removing heat.

questions.question: What is evaporation?
questions.questionThai: การระเหยคืออะไร?
options: When a liquid becomes a gas
When a gas becomes a liquid
When a solid becomes a liquid
When a liquid becomes a solid
questions.explanation: Evaporation is when a liquid changes into a gas, usually when heated.

questions.question: How are particles arranged in a solid?
questions.questionThai: อนุภาคในของแข็งถูกจัดเรียงอย่างไร?
options: Very far apart and moving fast
Tightly packed and vibrating in place
questions.explanation: In solids, particles are tightly packed together and vibrate in place but don't move around freely.

questions.question: Why can liquids take the shape of their container?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมของเหลวสามารถเป็นรูปร่างของภาชนะได้?
options: Because particles can slide past each other
questions.explanation: Liquid particles can slide past each other, allowing the liquid to flow and take the shape of its container.

questions.question: Why do gases spread out to fill any container?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมก๊าซถึงแพร่กระจายเต็มภาชนะ?
options: Particles move fast and are far apart
questions.explanation: Gas particles move very fast in all directions and are far apart, so they spread out to fill any space.

questions.question: What is condensation?
questions.questionThai: การควบแน่นคืออะไร?
options: When a gas changes into a liquid
questions.explanation: Condensation is when a gas changes into a liquid, usually when it cools down.

questions.question: In the reading passage, what happened to the ice cream in the freezer?
questions.questionThai: ในบทอ่าน เกิดอะไรขึ้นกับไอศกรีมในช่องแช่แข็ง?
options: It froze from liquid to solid
questions.explanation: In the passage, the liquid ice cream mixture froze into solid ice cream in the freezer.

questions.question: Where did the water droplets on Lek's cold glass come from?
questions.questionThai: หยดน้ำบนแก้วเย็นของเล็กมาจากไหน?
options: Water vapor in the air condensed on the cold glass
questions.explanation: Water vapor in the air cooled down when it touched the cold glass and condensed into liquid water droplets.

questions.question: Why can't you push your hand through a solid wall?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมคุณไม่สามารถดันมือผ่านกำแพงแข็งได้?
options: Because particles in solids are packed tightly together
questions.explanation: Particles in solids are packed so tightly that other objects cannot pass through them.

questions.question: Why do wet clothes dry on a clothesline?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมเสื้อผ้าเปียกแห้งบนราวตากผ้า?
options: The water evaporates into the air
questions.explanation: The sun and wind provide energy for the liquid water to evaporate and become water vapor in the air.

questions.question: What happens to particles when you heat a solid until it melts?
questions.questionThai: เกิดอะไรขึ้นกับอนุภาคเมื่อคุณให้ความร้อนของแข็งจนละลาย?
options: Particles gain energy, move more, and break free from fixed positions
questions.explanation: Heat gives particles more energy. They vibrate faster, break free from their fixed positions, and can slide past each other.

questions.question: Why is water unique among common substances?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมน้ำถึงมีเอกลักษณ์เฉพาะในบรรดาสารทั่วไป?
options: It only exists as a liquid
It's the only substance that is blue
It naturally exists as solid, liquid, and gas on Earth
questions.explanation: Water naturally exists in all three states on Earth (ice, liquid water, water vapor), which is unusual for common substances.

questions.question: Why does adding salt to ice make it melt faster?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมการเติมเกลือลงในน้ำแข็งทำให้มันละลายเร็วขึ้น?
options: Salt lowers the freezing point, so ice melts at a lower temperature
questions.explanation: Salt lowers the freezing point of water, meaning ice can melt even at temperatures that would normally keep it frozen.

questions.question: In the reading passage, why did Grandmother say changes in states are 'physical changes'?
questions.questionThai: ในบทอ่าน ทำไมคุณยายถึงบอกว่าการเปลี่ยนสถานะเป็น 'การเปลี่ยนแปลงทางกายภาพ'?
options: Because the matter changes into something new
Because the matter is still the same substance, just in a different form
questions.explanation: Physical changes don't create new substances. Water is still water whether it's ice, liquid, or steam - just in different forms.
